title: SSC CGL 2026 Sectional Timing Tier I 7 7 20260522

questions.question: This question is based on the following words. EVIL GONE STAY NEAR If the first letter of each word is removed, which of the following will form new meaningful English word?
questions.explanation: The first letter of each word is removed: VIL: Has no meaning ONE: Has a meaning (the lowest cardinal number) TAY: Has no meaning EAR: Has a meaning (is a part of the body) So, both GONE and NEAR will form a new meaningful English word.

questions.question: The sentence below has a word in which the letters are jumbled up. Rearrange the letters of that word, written in capital letters, to form the correct word. One ancient story that sheds light on the dark night is the ATEL of the hero swallowed by a huge fish.
questions.explanation: The word related to Story from the above options is TALE. Thus, the sentence will be - One ancient story that sheds light on the dark night is the TALE of the hero swallowed by a huge fish.

questions.question: Pick the odd one out. $R16, &U19, $S17, %Y23, +K8
questions.explanation: Here, the pattern is: R - 2 = 16 U - 2 = 19 S - 2 = 17 Y - 2 = 23 K - 2 = 9 So, option (b) is odd from the rest.

questions.question: A word is represented by only set of numbers as given in any one of the alternatives. The sets of numbers given in the alternatives are represented by two classes of alphabets as shown in the given two matrices. The columns and rows of Matrix-I are numbered from 0 to 4 and that of Matrix-II are numbered from 5 to 9. A letter from these matrices can be represented first by its row and next by its column, for example, 'S' can be represented by 14, 32 etc, and 'K' can be represented by 55, 87 etc. Similarly, you have to identify the set for the word 'VORTEX'.Matrix - IMatrix - II 0123456789 0SAVER5KFXOT 1VERAS6XOTFK 2RSEVA7TKFXO 3AVSRE8OXKTF 4ERASV9FTOKX
questions.explanation: Option (a):106613850258 VOAOVO So, option (a) is incorrect. Option (b):447904673499 VORTEX So, option (b) is correct. Option (c):235621794165 VFSORX So, option (c) is incorrect. Option (d):029833562087 VKRFRK So, option (d) is incorrect.

questions.question: When 212 is added to 5 times a natural number, the result is 337. What is the square root of the original number?
questions.explanation: Let, the number = N 5N + 212 = 337 5N = 125 N = 25 Square root of the number = √25 = 5

questions.question: Which two signs should be interchanged to make the below equation mathematically correct? 119 + 32 - 225 ÷ 25 X 9 = 168

Cost of drink A per litre is ⅗ of the cost of drink B per litre. A man wanted to buy certain quantity of drink A and 8 litres of drink B. But by mistake, the quantity of drink A and drink B got exchanged so the man is ended up paying 40% more. How much quantity of drink A did he wants to buy?
questions.explanation: Cost of drink B per litre = ₹5a Cost of drink A per litre = ₹3a Let the quantity of drink A bought by man = 'b' litres Original cost = b*3a + 8*5a = a(3b + 40) Cost after exchange = 8*3a + b*5a = a(24 + 5b) Now, a(24 + 5b) = 140% of a(3b + 40) 5(24 + 5b) = 7(3b + 40) 4b = 160 b = 40 So, the quantity of drink A he wants to buy = 40 litres

questions.question: Which option similarly pairs a physical quantity with its SI unit? Energy : Joule.
questions.explanation: Temperature is measured in Kelvin (SI unit). A Pascal is a unit of pressure, not force. A Watt is a unit of power, not length. A Volt is a unit of electric potential, not current. Thus, D is the correct pairing.

questions.question: In a merit list of 78 shortlisted candidates, Mira is ranked 20th from the top. If exactly 9 candidates are ranked between Mira and Neel, what is the sum of Neel's possible ranks from the top?
questions.explanation: Mira's rank from top = 20 If Neel is above Mira, Neel's rank = 20 − 9 − 1 = 10 If Neel is below Mira, Neel's rank = 20 + 9 + 1 = 30 Required sum = 10 + 30 = 40

questions.question: Here some problem or solution of the problem is given in the statement you have to choose the appropriate logical deduction for given statement. Statement: A new flyover was opened to reduce congestion at a busy junction. For the first month, delays decreased, but within six months, peak-hour delays returned close to earlier levels as more commuters started using private cars on that route. Transport experts recommend dedicated bus lanes and stricter parking controls near the junction to reduce repeated crowding. Conclusions: I. Building a flyover alone may not permanently reduce congestion. II. More road capacity can encourage higher private-car usage on the same corridor. III. All flyovers are useless and should never be constructed. Which conclusion(s) necessarily follow(s)?
questions.explanation: Conclusion I follows because congestion reduced briefly but then returned close to earlier levels. Conclusion II follows because the statement connects the return of delays with more commuters shifting to private cars on that route. Conclusion III does not follow because the statement talks about one flyover case and does not justify "all" and "never."

questions.question: Select the number from among the given options that can replace the question mark (?) in the following series. 2, 3, 6, 15, 42, 123, ?
Which of the following letter-clusters will replace the question mark (?) in the given series? AY, BW, DU, GS, ?
questions.explanation: First letters increase with growing steps: +1, +2, +3, +4: A→B→D→G→K. Second letters decrease by 2 each time: Y→W→U→S→Q. So next pair is KQ.

questions.question: On an assembly line, 41 parts are kept in a single row. Part X is 24th from the left end and Part Y is 6th from the right end. How many parts are between X and Y?
questions.explanation: Let N = total parts = 41 Position of Y from the left = N − 6 + 1 = 41 − 6 + 1 = 36 Parts between X and Y = 36 − 24 − 1 = 11

questions.question: Which of the following letter-number clusters will replace the question mark (?) in the given series to make it logically complete? GP 12 HQ 24 IR 36 JS 48 KT 60 ?
questions.explanation: GP12 HQ24 IR36 JS48 KT60 LU72 So, option (d) is the correct answer.

questions.question: Find the missing number: 8 : 145 :: 11 : ?
questions.explanation: Let n be the number. Required number = n² + (n + 1)² For n = 8 = 8² + 9² = 64 + 81 = 145 For n = 11 = 11² + 12² = 121 + 144 = 265

questions.question: Find the missing number: 3 : 80 :: 5 : ?
questions.explanation: The relation follows the pattern n⁴ − 1. 80 = 3⁴ − 1 = 81 − 1 = 80 So, required number = 5⁴ − 1 = 625 − 1 = 624

questions.question: Study the following information and answer the given question: 'G + H' means G is the maternal grandfather of H. 'G ? H' means G is the son of H. 'G | H' means G is the father of H. 'G { H' means G is the sister of H. How is Z related to A, if in the expression, 'A ? H + I { V ? O | N', N is the daughter of Z?
questions.explanation: ![](https://u1.oliveboard.in/exams/solution/oliveimg/p30420/image032.jpg) It is given that, 'G + H' means G is the maternal grandfather of H. 'G ? H' means G is the son of H. 'G | H' means G is the father of H. 'G { H' means G is the sister of H. After decoding the given expression, we get: A ? H + I { V ? O | N A is the son of H. H is the maternal grandfather of I. I is the sister of V. V is the son of O. O is the father of N. ![](https://u1.oliveboard.in/exams/solution/oliveimg/p30420/image033.jpg) N is the daughter of Z. ![](https://u1.oliveboard.in/exams/solution/oliveimg/p30420/image034.jpg) So, Z is the sister of A.

questions.question: In a certain code language, 'GRAPEVINE' is coded as 'HTDQGYHMD'. In the same language, 'JACKFRUIT' is coded as ____.
questions.explanation: Here, the logic is: G + 1 = H R + 2 = T A + 3 = D P + 1 = Q E + 2 = G V + 3 = Y I - 1 = H N - 1 = M E - 1 = D Similarly, J + 1 = K A + 2 = C C + 3 = F K + 1 = L F + 2 = H R + 3 = U U - 1 = T I - 1 = H T - 1 = S

questions.question: A is the sister of F, who is the mother of B. E is the daughter of C. D is the maternal grandmother of B. C is the daughter-in-law of F. There is only one male member in a family. How is E related to B?
questions.explanation: ![](https://u1.oliveboard.in/exams/solution/oliveimg/p30453/image001.jpg) A is the sister of F, who is the mother of B. D is the maternal grandmother of B. ![](https://u1.oliveboard.in/exams/solution/oliveimg/p30453/image004.jpg) E is the daughter of C. C is the daughter-in-law of F. ![](https://u1.oliveboard.in/exams/solution/oliveimg/p30453/image005.jpg) So, E is the daughter of B.

questions.question: A person walks 12km to north, then 5km west, then 7km south, and finally 9km to his left. In which direction is he facing now?
questions.explanation: A person walks 12km to north, then 5km west, then 7km south, and finally 9 km to his left. ![](https://u1.oliveboard.in/exams/solution/oliveimg/p30453/image017.jpg) So, he is facing in east direction.

questions.question: Select the figure from among the given options that can replace the question mark (?) in the following series. ![](https://u1.oliveboard.in/exams/solution/oliveimg/p20574/image036.jpg)
questions.explanation: The edges of the middle overlapping figure are increasing by one in each step. The two numbers in the first step figure are get multiplied and written opposite to the highest number placed in second to the step. The star figure is rotated 90 ![](https://u1.oliveboard.in/exams/solution/oliveimg/p20574/image037.png) anticlockwise direction. The '!' symbol is increasing and decreasing by one alternately in each step also it is shifting its position in increasing order. Thus, the answer is - ![](https://u1.oliveboard.in/exams/solution/oliveimg/p20574/image038.jpg)

questions.question: Select the combination of letters that when sequentially placed in the blanks of the given series will complete the series. _ 2 1 3 1 _ 1 5 1 _ 1 7 _ 8 1 9
questions.explanation: Here, in the given series, the alternate number is increasing by 1. So, the complete sequence is 1 2 1 3 1 4 1 5 1 6 1 7 1 8 1 9

questions.question: A statement is given followed by two inferences I and II. You have to consider the statement to be true even if it seems to be at variance from commonly known facts. You have to decide which of the given inferences, if any, follow from the given statement. Statement: Consuming too much added sugar over a long period of time can lead to blood sugar crashes, faster ageing, and an increased risk of obesity, heart disease, diabetes, or cognitive decline. It is also additive in nature. Inferences: I. Added sugar is unhealthy for the body. II. All diseases that are harmful are caused by added sugar.
options: If both I and II follow
If neither I nor II follows
questions.explanation: The given statement states that, Long-term overconsumption of added sugar can cause blood sugar falls, accelerate ageing, and raise the risk of diabetes, heart disease, obesity, and cognitive decline. In nature, it is also additive. I. Added sugar is unhealthy for the body: Added sugar leads to blood sugar falls, accelerate ageing, and raise the risk of diabetes, heart disease, obesity, and cognitive decline. So, it is unhealthy in nature. So, (I) follows. II. All diseases that are harmful are caused by added sugar: Few harmful diseases are caused by added sugar. We cannot say that sugar is responsible for all harmful diseases. So, (II) does not follow.

questions.question: A coding rule is followed: Roll numbers ending with an even digit belong to Batch 1. Roll numbers ending with an odd digit belong to Batch 2. Identify the roll numbers for Batch 1 and Batch 2:
questions.explanation: Option A: P4526 (even -> Batch 1), Q7315 (odd -> Batch 2) Option B: R2481 (odd -> Batch 2), S5625 (odd -> Batch 2) Option C: M8391 (odd -> Batch 2), N6743 (odd -> Batch 2) Option D: T9105 (odd -> Batch 2), U8459 (odd -> Batch 2)

questions.question: Consider the following statements regarding Indo-Islamic architecture during the Sultanate period: 1) Use of true arches and domes was introduced on a large scale during this period. 2) Minarets were primarily used for defensive military purposes. 3). Decorative calligraphy became an important architectural element. Which of the statements is/are correct?
questions.explanation: • Statement 1 is correct: The Sultanate period introduced the Arcuate style, characterized by the use of true arches (using a keystone) and domes, replacing the traditional Indian Trabeate style (beams and lintels). • Statement 2 is incorrect: Minarets were primarily used as a call to prayer (Adhan) or as a tower of victory (e.g., Qutub Minar), rather than for defensive military purposes. • Statement 3 is correct: Since Islamic tradition avoided the representation of human or animal figures in religious spaces, architects used Calligraphy (Kufic script), Arabesque (geometric patterns), and floral designs for decoration.

questions.question: Which Article empowers the Supreme Court to issue writs for enforcement of Fundamental Rights?
questions.explanation: • Article 32 is known as the "Right to Constitutional Remedies." It empowers the Supreme Court to issue writs (Habeas Corpus, Mandamus, Prohibition, Quo-Warranto, and Certiorari) for the enforcement of Fundamental Rights. Dr. B.R. Ambedkar famously called this Article the "Heart and Soul of the Constitution." • Note on Article 226: This Article gives similar powers to the High Courts, but the question specifically asks for the Supreme Court.

questions.question: In July 2025, who was appointed as the new Chief Executive Officer (CEO) of the International Cricket Council (ICC)?
questions.explanation: Indian broadcaster Sanjog Gupta has been appointed as the new Chief Executive Officer of the International Cricket Council (ICC). Announcing his appointment, the ICC Chairman Jay Shah said that Mr Gupta will bring extensive experience in sports strategy and commercialisation, which will be invaluable for the ICC. He further said that the Nominations Committee of the ICC unanimously recommended Sanjog Gupta after considering several exceptional candidates for the post of CEO. Gupta, who started his career as a journalist, later served at several broadcasting entities and scaled up his sports portfolio with multi-language, digital-first and women-focused coverage.

questions.question: Fill in the blank: Restrictions on the freedom of trade, commerce and intercourse between one State and another in public interest can be imposed by Parliament under Article ______ of the Indian Constitution.
questions.explanation: Article 302 empowers Parliament to impose such restrictions on this freedom as may be required in the public interest. Article 301 declares that trade, commerce, and intercourse throughout the territory of India shall be free. Article 304 deals with the power of State Legislatures to impose restrictions (subject to Presidential sanction).

questions.question: Which classical text is attributed to Matanga?
questions.explanation: • Brihaddeshi is a landmark Sanskrit text on Indian classical music attributed to Matanga Muni (approx. 8th century). It is historically significant as the first text to define the word Raga. • Natya Shastra is attributed to Bharata Muni. • Sangita Ratnakara was written by Sarngadeva. • Chaturdandi Prakashika was written by Venkatamakhin.

questions.question: What is the most distinctive feature of the Garba dance of Gujarat?
options: Solo expressions performed in temple courtyards
Circular group dance around a symbolic lamp
questions.explanation: • The most distinctive feature of Garba is its performance in a circle, representing the Hindu view of time as cyclical. • Dancers move around a centrally placed Garba Deep (a perforated earthen lamp) or an image of the Goddess Shakti. • The lamp symbolizes the "embryo" or the life within the womb, celebrating the feminine form of divinity.

questions.question: The Lui-Ngai-Ni festival is celebrated as:
options: A seed-sowing festival of Naga tribes
A New Year festival of Mizoram
A river worship festival of Meghalaya
questions.explanation: • Lui Ngai Ni is the seed-sowing festival celebrated by the Naga tribes of Manipur India. • The festival heralds the season of seed sowing and marks the start of the year for the Nagas and the festival was declared a state holiday since 1988. • It is a major cultural event that brings together various Naga tribes like the Tangkhul, Mao, and Maram.

questions.question: Which SCO member country was the most recent to join the organisation?
questions.explanation: • Belarus was admitted as a new member at the SCO Summit in Astana on 4 July 2024. • The Shanghai Cooperation Organization (SCO) is a ten-member multilateral organization, established on 15 June 2001 by the leaders of Kazakhstan, China, Kyrgyzstan, Russia, Tajikistan and Uzbekistan. • These countries, except Uzbekistan, had been members of the Shanghai-5 group formed in April 1996. In 2001, Uzbekistan was admitted as a member (thus transforming it into the Shanghai-6). • At the July 2005 Astana Summit, India, Iran and Pakistan were granted Observer status. In July 2015 in Ufa, Russia, the SCO decided to admit India (and Pakistan) as full Member. • In June 2017, at the Summit in Astana, India (and Pakistan) officially joined SCO as a fullmember. • Iran was granted the status of a Member State at the SCO Summit hosted virtually by India on 4 July 2023. • Apart from the 10 Members, the SCO has 2 Observers (Afghanistan and Mongolia) and 14 Dialogue Partners (Azerbaijan, Armenia, Cambodia, Nepal, Turkey, Sri Lanka, United Arab Emirates, Kuwait, Maldives, Bahrain, Myanmar, Egypt, Saudi Arabia and Qatar). As per the SCO Charter, Russian and Chinese are the only two official languages of the SCO.

questions.question: Which of the following statements regarding India's Olympic history is incorrect?
options: Abhinav Bindra won India's first-ever individual Olympic gold medal in the 10-meter air rifle event at the 2008 Beijing Games.
The Indian Men's Hockey team has won a total of 8 Olympic gold medals, the most by any country in the sport.
Karnam Malleswari became the first Indian woman to win an Olympic medal when she secured a bronze in weightlifting at the 2000 Sydney Olympics.
Neeraj Chopra is the only Indian athlete to win two individual Olympic medals.
questions.explanation: Statement d is Incorrect: While Neeraj Chopra did win a Gold (Tokyo 2020) and a Silver (Paris 2024), he is not the only Indian to win two individual medals. Neeraj Chopra is, however, the first Indian to win two individual medals in Track and Field (Athletics). Others who have two individual Olympic medals: Sushil Kumar (Wrestling: 2008 Bronze, 2012 Silver) P.V. Sindhu (Badminton: 2016 Silver, 2020 Bronze) Manu Bhaker (Shooting: Two Bronze medals in the same edition, Paris 2024) Statement a is Correct: In 2008, Abhinav Bindra created history by winning the Gold in the 10m Air Rifle event. Before this, all of India's gold medals had come from the Men's Hockey team. Statement b is Correct: Field Hockey is India's most successful Olympic sport. The team won six consecutive gold medals between 1928 and 1956, and two more in 1964 and 1980. Statement c is Correct: At the 2000 Sydney Olympics, weightlifter Karnam Malleswari won the bronze medal in the 69kg category, marking a historic moment as the first-ever medal won by an Indian woman.

questions.question: Which of the following statements correctly explains the law of conservation of energy?
options: Energy can be created or destroyed depending upon the type of physical process involved.
Energy can neither be created nor destroyed but can be transformed from one form to another, and the total energy of an isolated system remains constant.
Energy remains conserved only in mechanical systems and not in thermal or electrical systems.
Energy conversion always results in a net gain of usable energy in a closed system.
questions.explanation: • "Energy can neither be created nor destroyed but can be transformed from one form to another, and the total energy of an isolated system remains constant" is the fundamental definition of the First Law of Thermodynamics. • In an isolated system, while energy can change its form (e.g., potential to kinetic, or electrical to heat), the total quantity of energy remains constant. • Statement (d) is incorrect because energy conversions usually result in a net loss of usable energy (due to entropy), not a gain.

questions.question: Consider the following statements regarding antigens: Statement 1: Antigens are substances that can specifically bind to antibodies or T-cell receptors and trigger an immune response. Statement 2: All antigens are proteins produced only by pathogenic microorganisms. Which of the statements is/are correct?
options: Both Statement 1 and Statement 2 are correct
Neither Statement 1 nor Statement 2 is correct
questions.explanation: • Statement 1 is correct: Antigens are defined by their ability to be recognized by the adaptive immune system (specifically B-cell antibodies or T-cell receptors) and their potential to stimulate an immune response. • Statement 2 is incorrect: Not all antigens are proteins; they can also be polysaccharides (carbohydrates), lipids, or nucleic acids. Furthermore, they are not produced only by pathogens. Antigens can be found in pollen, egg whites, transplanted organs, or even on our own red blood cells (self-antigens like the A/B/Rh factors).

questions.question: Which of the following statements about the Viksit Bharat-Guarantee for Rozgar and Ajeevika Mission (Gramin) Act, 2025 (VB-G RAM G Act) are correct? 1. The Act replaces the Mahatma Gandhi National Rural Employment Guarantee Act (MGNREGA) and increases the statutory employment guarantee from 100 days to 125 days per rural household each year. 2. The Act emphasises linking wage employment with creation of durable rural infrastructure across priority areas like water security, core rural infrastructure, livelihood-related works and climate resilience. 3. Under the new financial architecture, the state must bear all the cost through a normative allocation framework, creating stronger incentives for effective implementation and preventing misuse.
questions.explanation: ✘ Statement 3 is incorrect - Under the new architecture, states share both cost and responsibility through a normative allocation framework, creating stronger incentives for effective implementation and preventing misuse. Planning is grounded in regional realities through Gram Panchayat Plans. The shift from a central sector scheme to a centrally sponsored framework reflects the inherently local nature of rural employment and asset creation. Normative allocation vs demand-based funding: Normative allocation refers to the allocation of funds by the Central Government to States based on predefined norms. The earlier demand-based funding model often resulted in unpredictable fund allocations and mismatches in budgeting. Normative funding aligns the scheme with the budgeting framework followed by most Government of India schemes. The shift to normative funding does not dilute the employment guarantee provided under the scheme. Funds are allocated using objective and transparent parameters, ensuring predictable and rational financial planning. The legal entitlement to employment or unemployment allowance is fully preserved under the normative funding system. Further insights: The Viksit Bharat-Guarantee for Rozgar and Ajeevika Mission (Gramin) Act, 2025 (commonly VB-G RAM G) is a new rural employment law enacted by the Indian Parliament. It replaces the Mahatma Gandhi National Rural Employment Guarantee Act (MGNREGA), 2005 to modernise and strengthen rural employment frameworks. The Act aligns rural job guarantees with the Government's long-term development vision of Viksit Bharat @2047. Key Features 1. Enhanced Employment Guarantee - Provides a statutory guarantee of at least 125 days of wage employment per rural household per financial year for adult members who volunteer for unskilled manual work - up from 100 days under MGNREGA. - Allows a maximum 60-day pause in work during peak agricultural seasons (like sowing and harvesting) to ensure labour availability for farming. 2. Integration with Rural Development & Infrastructure - Employment is linked with priority work categories such as: Water security and related works Core rural infrastructure Livelihood-related infrastructure Climate resilience and disaster-mitigation works - Assets created are aggregated through a "Viksit Bharat National Rural Infrastructure Stack" for coordinated rural development planning. 3. Planning, Governance & Monitoring - Viksit Gram Panchayat Plans are to be prepared at the village level and integrated with national infrastructure systems (e.g., PM Gati Shakti). - Administrative expenditure limits are increased to strengthen staffing, training, technical support, and programme delivery. - Digital monitoring (e.g., geo-tagging, biometric attendance) and social audits are mandated for transparency. 4. Funding & Financial Structure - The law transitions from a central-sector model (where the Centre funded most costs) to a centrally sponsored framework with normative allocations. - Cost-sharing is generally 60:40 between Centre and states (with 90:10 for North Eastern/Himalayan states, and 100% Centre funding for Union Territories). - Predictable normative financing is intended to support sound budgeting and planning. 5. Rights & Benefits - Workers receive weekly or timely wage payments (within a fortnight of work done). - A statutory unemployment allowance applies if work is not provided within stipulated timeframes. - Enhanced guarantees aim to increase rural incomes, reduce distress migration, and make employment more resilient and productive. Comparison: MGNREGA vs Viksit BharatG RAM G Aspect MGNREGA Viksit Bharat-G RAM G Guaranteed wage employment 100 days of wage employment per rural household 125 days of wage employment per rural household Nature of works Multiple and scattered categories of works with limited strategic focus 4 clearly defined priority areas Focus areas Broad, non-prioritised works Water security, rural infrastructure, livelihoods, climate resilience Sharing of wage costs Centre bears unskilled wage costs; states bear unemployment allowance State cost-sharing: 60:40 for most states; 90:10 for special-category states Pause in execution of work No explicit statutory "pause window" States can notify up to 60 days in a financial year when work will not be executed Funding pattern Demand-based funding with unpredictable allocations Normative funding ensuring predictable budgeting while protecting employment guarantee Planning approach Gram Panchayat planning is central Integrates institutionalised convergence and infrastructure planning

questions.question: Read the following statements carefully and choose the correct option regarding Assertion (A) and Reason (R). Assertion (A): As part of the 1991 economic reforms, the policy of industrial licensing was abolished for most industries in India. Reason (R): The objective of this measure was to reduce government control and promote competition and efficiency in the industrial sector.
options: Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A.
Both A and R are true but R is not the correct explanation of A.
A is true but R is false.
A is false but R is true.
questions.explanation: • Assertion (A) is true: The New Economic Policy (NEP) of 1991 introduced Liberalization. One of its core pillars was the abolition of industrial licensing (except for a short list of 18 industries related to security, strategic concerns, or environmental hazards). • Reason (R) is true: Before 1991, the "License Raj" created a slow, bureaucratic environment. The reform aimed to remove these "red tape" barriers to allow private players to enter markets easily, thereby fostering competition, modernization, and economic efficiency. • Since R explains why the policy in A was enacted, it is the correct explanation.

questions.question: Consider the following statements about Harappan bronze-casting: Statement I: The Harappans used the 'lost wax' technique to create bronze statues, in which a wax model was covered with clay, melted out, and replaced with molten metal. Statement II: Among Harappan bronze figures, the most famous human figure is the "Dancing Girl," while notable animal figures include the buffalo and the goat.
options: Both Statement I and Statement II are correct and Statement II correctly explains Statement I.
Both Statement I and Statement II are correct, but Statement II does not explain Statement I.
Statement I is correct, but Statement II is incorrect.
Statement I is incorrect, but Statement II is correct.
questions.explanation: • Statement I is correct: The Harappans were pioneers in metallurgy. They used the cire perdue or "lost wax" process. This involved creating a wax model, coating it in clay, heating it to let the wax flow out through a small hole, and then pouring molten bronze into the hollow cavity. • Statement II is correct: The "Dancing Girl" (found in Mohenjo-daro) is indeed the most iconic human bronze sculpture of this civilization. Additionally, the Harappans produced various bronze animals, with the buffalo (with its swept-back horns) and the goat being prominent examples of their artistic skill. • While both statements are factually accurate, Statement II is not an explanation for Statement I. Statement I describes a technical process, whereas Statement II provides examples of the finished products. The existence of the "Dancing Girl" does not explain how or why the lost wax technique works; it simply serves as an application of that technique.

questions.question: Consider the following statements about the long jump: 1) The take-off board must be placed at least 1 meter from the landing pit. 2) The landing area is usually filled with soft sand. Which of the statements is/are correct?
questions.explanation: Statement 1 is correct: In competitive long jump (IAAF/World Athletics standards), the take-off board is placed between 1 to 3 meters from the edge of the landing pit to ensure the athlete has sufficient space to land safely. Statement 2 is correct: The landing area (the "pit") is filled with fine, damp sand that is leveled before each jump. The sand serves to cushion the impact and allows officials to measure the mark made by the athlete's landing.

questions.question: Which curve shows the minimum combinations of inputs that produce a given level of output?
questions.explanation: • An Isoquant (or "Equal Product Curve") represents all the combinations of two inputs (usually labor and capital) that yield the same level of output. • Indifference curves relate to consumer preferences (utility). • Budget constraints show what a consumer can afford. • Average cost curves relate to the cost per unit of output.

questions.question: Which government scheme aims to provide assured pension benefits to workers in the unorganised sector after the age of 60 years?
options: Pradhan Mantri Jeevan Jyoti Bima Yojana (PMJJBY)
questions.explanation: The Pradhan Mantri Shram Yogi Maan-Dhan (PM-SYM) is the government scheme designed to provide an assured monthly pension of ₹3,000 to workers in the unorganised sector after they reach 60 years of age. It is a voluntary, contributory pension scheme with a 1:1 matching contribution from the central government. Target Group: Unorganised workers (e.g., street vendors, domestic workers, home-based workers) aged 18 to 40 years with a monthly income of ₹15,000 or less. Pension Benefit: A guaranteed minimum pension of ₹3,000 per month after age 60. Family Pension: Upon the death of the beneficiary, the spouse is entitled to 50% of the pension (₹1,500 per month). Contribution: The worker's contribution varies based on age (ranging from ₹55 to ₹200 per month), with the government contributing an equal amount.

questions.question: Which of the following books is NOT written by Rabindranath Tagore?
questions.explanation: • Gitanjali, Gora, and The Home and the World (Ghare-Baire) are among the most famous works of Rabindranath Tagore. • An Era of Darkness: The British Empire in India was written by Shashi Tharoor (published in 2016). It provides a critical account of the impact of British colonial rule on India.

questions.question: While India ranks sixth overall in the Asia Manufacturing Index (AMI) 2026, it scores poorly in several areas. Which of the following is NOT among the weak spots identified for India in this report?
questions.explanation: India ranked sixth among 11 Asian countries in the newly released Asia Manufacturing Index (AMI) 2026, which measures the relative competitiveness of major manufacturing economies across eight key pillars and 43 sub-parameters. In the inaugural 2024 report, India ranked fourth among eight countries. It slipped to sixth in 2025 when the study expanded to 11 countries - a position it retains in the 2026 index. There is, however, good news. India's economy is clearly bustling, ranking third overall ' behind only Vietnam and China. It scores the highest on economic growth, a key sub-parameter under the economy pillar, and ranks second on the economic scale, trailing only China. This growth is supported by workforce strength. India ranks first among the 11 countries, measured across sub-parameters including labour force size and growth, demographic structure, labour costs, and educational attainment. India ranks fourth on infrastructure and fifth on innovation ' areas expected to improve further with a sustained government push. Still, several weak spots stand out. India ranks ninth on tax policy, international trade, and political risk. It posts the lowest score on tax rates among the 11 countries and lags behind China, Indonesia, Malaysia, Vietnam (the top scorer), and Thailand on tax incentives. On international trade, India scores poorly on parameters such as free trade agreement integration, where China, Indonesia, Japan, Malaysia, Singapore, and Vietnam perform much better. It also ranks low on logistics performance, with six countries ahead of it and Singapore leading the category. Further insights: The index ranks countries across eight broad pillars: economy, political risk, business environment, international trade, tax policy, infrastructure, workforce, and innovation. Each pillar is broken down into sub-parameters and scored on a scale of 100, with higher scores indicating stronger performance. In its third year, the Index ' released by Dezan Shira & Associates, a pan-Asian market entry advisory firm headquartered in Hong Kong ' places India behind key Asian competitors in the global manufacturing race. Malaysia has moved up to second place for the first time in 2026, replacing Vietnam, which has slipped to third. Among developed economies, Singapore has climbed one rank to fourth, overtaking South Korea, which has dropped to fifth. These shifts, the study says, reflect intensifying regional competition. India remains ahead of Indonesia (seventh), while Thailand has made a sharp climb ' from 10th in 2025 to eighth in 2026. The Philippines now sits at 10th place, Japan ranks ninth, and China continues to dominate the index, holding the top position for the third consecutive year. Bangladesh ranks last among the 11 countries studied.

questions.question: Consider the following statements regarding tropical cyclones: 1) Tropical cyclones develop only over warm ocean waters with sea surface temperatures above 26°C. 2) They weaken rapidly over land due to loss of moisture supply. 3) The Coriolis force is essential for their initial formation. 4) Tropical cyclones are associated with high atmospheric pressure at the centre. Which of the statements given above are correct?
questions.explanation: • Statement 1 is correct: Tropical cyclones require warm tropical oceans with a sea surface temperature (SST) greater than 26.5°C to provide the necessary thermal energy. • Statement 2 is correct: Once a cyclone reaches land (landfall), it loses its main energy source (latent heat of condensation from sea moisture) and the increased surface friction further weakens it. • Statement 3 is correct: The Coriolis force is necessary to create the rotational motion (vorticity). This is why cyclones do not form very close to the Equator ($0^\circ$ to $5^\circ$ latitude), where the Coriolis force is negligible. • Statement 4 is incorrect: Tropical cyclones are characterized by extremely low atmospheric pressure at the center (the "Eye"). It is this low-pressure gradient that causes winds to spiral inward.

questions.question: What phenomenon causes mirages in deserts or hot roads?
options: Refraction of light due to temperature gradient
questions.explanation: • A mirage occurs due to Total Internal Reflection and refraction. On hot days, the air near the ground is much hotter (and less dense) than the air above it. • As light from the sky travels downward, it passes through layers of increasing temperature, causing it to bend (refract) increasingly away from the normal until it undergoes total internal reflection. • To the observer, the light appears to come from the ground, creating the illusion of a pool of water reflecting the sky.

questions.question: Which of the following rivers forms the boundary between the states of Madhya Pradesh and Maharashtra for a considerable stretch of its course?
questions.explanation: • The Wainganga River originates in the Mahadeo Hills of the Satpura Range in Madhya Pradesh. • As it flows south toward Maharashtra (eventually joining the Wardha to become the Pranhita), it forms the boundary between the two states for a significant stretch. • While the Narmada and Tapi also touch these states, the Wainganga's southern course creates amore defined "considerable stretch" of border in the eastern Vidarbha region.

questions.question: Arrange the following events in chronological order: 1) Simon Commission 2) Government of India Act, 1935 3) Nehru Report 4) Civil Disobedience Movement
questions.explanation: Simon Commission (November 1927): Established to review the working of the 1919 Act. Nehru Report (August 1928): Prepared by an all-party committee chaired by Motilal Nehru as a response to the Simon Commission. Civil Disobedience Movement (March 1930): Launched with the Dandi March to protest the salt tax. Government of India Act, 1935 (August 1935): Passed by the British Parliament, largely based on the reports of the Simon Commission and the Round Table Conferences.

questions.question: Consider the following statements regarding the Seventh Schedule of the Indian Constitution: Statement 1: Public order, police and prisons are included in the State List. Statement 2: Education is exclusively mentioned in the Union List under the Seventh Schedule. Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
questions.explanation: • Statement 1 is correct: Under the Seventh Schedule, Public Order, Police, and Prisons are subjects listed under the State List (List II). This gives state governments the primary power to legislate and manage these sectors. • Statement 2 is incorrect: Education is not exclusively in the Union List. Originally it was in the State List, but via the 42nd Amendment Act of 1976, it was moved to the Concurrent List (List III), meaning both the Centre and the States can legislate on it.

questions.question: Which of the following statements best describes Binod Kumar Pasayat's achievement?
options: He was a noted Sambalpuri lyricist and poet who was also a chef by profession.
In recognition of his lifelong contributions to literature and culture, he was awarded the Padma Shri in 2024.
Over his seven-decade career, he received more than 45 honours, including the Sarala Puraskar (2010) and Odisha Sahitya Akademi Puraskar (2015).
He served as the first Chairman of the Odisha Sahitya Akademi.
questions.explanation: Binod Kumar Pasayat, a renowned Sambalpuri lyricist, playwright and poet, passed away at the age of 90 while undergoing treatment in Sambalpur, Odisha in August 2025. He had been suffering from age-related ailments. Born on December 3, 1935, in Kusmel village, Balangir district, Pasayat was originally a barber by profession but became a celebrated figure in Sambalpuri and Koshali literature. He was introduced to drama at age 10 and later moved to Sambalpur in 1953, where he opened a barber shop that also became a space for his writing. Pasayat contributed significantly to culture with popular songs like "Ae Nani Sulochana" and "Rathar Chaka Chale Ghidighidi", and wrote songs for Odia films such as Samarpan and Para Stri. His plays like Ukhi, Mui Nai Mare and Chinha are considered major works in Sambalpuri theatre. In recognition of his lifelong contributions to literature and culture, he was awarded the Padma Shri in 2024. Over his seven-decade career, he received more than 45 honours, including the Sarala Puraskar (2008) and Odisha Sahitya Akademi Puraskar (2010). Despite national acclaim, he continued his ancestral profession as a barber until ill health prevented him from doing so. Prominent leaders including Union Minister Dharmendra Pradhan and Odisha Chief Minister Mohan Charan Majhi expressed condolences on his passing. Further insights: Pt. Nilakantha Dash was the first President (Chairman) of the Odisha Sahitya Akademi, assuming office on September 9, 1957.

questions.question: Arun goes 6 km against the current of a stream in 1 hour, and 5 km along the current in 20 minutes. How long will it take him to go 21 km in stationary water?
questions.explanation: Upstream speed of Arun = 6/1 = 6 kmph Downstream speed of Arun = 5/ (⅓) = 15 kmph Speed of Arun in stationary water = (½) * [Downstream speed + upstream speed] = (½) * 21 = 10.5 kmph Time taken by him to cover 21 km in stationary water = 21/10.5 = 2 hours

questions.question: Find the square root of (16 - 6√7).
Find the value of [126 ÷ 3.5 + 8 * 6] ÷ 12 * 17.
Thirty men can build a road in 42 days. After 21 days, 18 more men join them. How many more days will it take to finish the road?
questions.explanation: Total work = 30 X 42 = 1260 man-days. Work done in 21 days by 30 men = 30 X 21 = 630 MD. Remaining = 1260 − 630 = 630 MD. Now 30 + 18 = 48 men. Days = 630 ÷ 48 = 13.125 days = 13⅛ days.

questions.question: The ratio of the area of a sector of a circle and the area of the circle is 1 : 4. If the area of circle is 154 cm², the perimeter of the sector of the circle will be.
questions.explanation: "Angle subtended by the arc of the sector of circle at its centre = 90° Area of circle = 154 ![](https://u1.oliveboard.in/exams/solution/oliveimg/stubeimg/b7a275b0dc440317182bce40e9636b27.png) "

questions.question: If tan φ = ¾, then find 5 sec φ − 2 cosec φ + 3 cos φ.
questions.explanation: tan φ = opposite/adjacent opposite = 3, adjacent = 4, hypotenuse = 5 sec φ = 5/4 cosec φ = 5/3 cos φ = ⅘ 5 sec φ - 2 cosec φ + 3 cos φ = 5 * (5/4) - 2 * (5/3) + 3 * (⅘) = 25/4 - 10/3 + 12/5 = (375 - 200 + 144)/60 = 319/60

questions.question: The marks obtained by 9 students are 30, 48, 60, 72, 28, 42, 36, 32, and 39. Later on, 7 grace marks were added to each student. What are the average marks of the revised marks of the students?
A square PQRS is inscribed in a circle of radius R. Another circle is inscribed in PQRS and a square LMNO is inscribed in this circle. The side LM is equal to
questions.explanation: Diagonal of square PQRS = diameter of circle = 2R Side of square PQRS √2 = 2R Side of square PQRS = 2R / √2 = R√2 Radius of circle inscribed in PQRS = (side of PQRS) / 2 = R√2 / 2 = R / √2 Diagonal of square LMNO = diameter of inner circle = 2 (R / √2) = √2 R Side of square LMNO √2 = √2 R Side of square LMNO = √2 R / √2 = R

questions.question: The cost price of a book is Rs. 1800 and A shopkeeper marked the book at 30% above the cost price. If two successive discounts of 20% and 10% are given, then find the Selling price of the book.
Find the area of a triangle whose vertices are P(5, 3), Q(7, 7) and R(1, 4). (in unit²)
questions.explanation: Coordinates of P = (x₁, y₁) = (5, 3) Coordinates of Q = (x₂, y₂) = (7, 7) Coordinates of R = (x₃, y₃) = (1, 4) Area of triangle PQR = ½*[x₁(y₂ - y₃) + x₂(y₃ - y₁) + x₃(y₁ - y₂)] = ½ * [5(7 - 4) + 7(4 - 3) + 1(3 - 7)] = ½ [15 + 7 - 4] = ½ * 18 = 9 unit²

questions.question: Worker A can complete a task in 18 days, while Worker B can complete the same task in 24 days. They work together for the first 6 days, after which A's efficiency is reduced to half, while B continues to work at the same rate. In how many total days will the task be completed?
questions.explanation: Let total work be LCM of 18 and 24 = 72 units Efficiency of A = 72/18 = 4 units per day Efficiency of B = 72/24 = 3 units per day Work done in 6 days = 6 x (4 + 3) = 42 units New efficiency of A = 2 units per day Remaining work = 72 - 42 = 30 units Time taken by A and B together to complete 30 units = 30/(2 + 5) = 6 days Total number of days = 6 + 6 = 12 days

questions.question: A sphere of radius 6 cm is recast into a cylinder of the same radius. Find the cylinder's height.
questions.explanation: Sphere volume = Cylinder volume (4/3)π X 6³ = π X 6² X h h = 8 cm

questions.question: What is the average of the squares of the first 20 positive integers?
questions.explanation: Sum of squares formula = n(n + 1)(2n + 1)/6 with n = 20 Compute numerator = 20 * 21 * 41 = 17,220 Sum of squares = 17,220 / 6 = 2,870 Average = 2,870 / 20 = 143.5

questions.question: Tina, Mina, and Rina enter into a partnership by investing in the ratio of 9: 8: 5. After one year, Mina invests another Rs. 300000 and Rina, at the end of 2 years, invests Rs. 150000. At the end of three years, profits are shared in the ratio of 3: 4: 2. Find the initial investment of each.
questions.explanation: Let, the initial investments of Tina, Mina and Rina be ₹9x, ₹8x and ₹5x respectively. Ratio of profit of Tina, Mina & Rina = (9x * 3) : (8x * 3 + 300000 * 2) : (5x * 3 + 150000 * 1) = 27x : (24x + 600000) : (15x + 150000) = 3 : 4 : 2 So, 27x / (24x + 600000) = 3 / 4 => 108x = 72x + 1800000 => 36x = 1800000 => x = 50000 Tina's initial investments = 9x = 9 50000 = ₹450000 Mina's initial investments = 8x = 8 50000 = ₹400000 Rina's initial investments = 5x = 5 50000 = ₹250000

questions.question: Difference of two numbers is 300. If 8% of one number is 12% of the other, find the two numbers.
questions.explanation: Let the numbers are x and y. Given, 8% of x = 12% of y => 8x = 12y => 2x = 3y => x = 1.5y. Also given, x − y = 300 => 1.5y − y = 300 => 0.5y = 300 => y = 600 => x = 900

questions.question: A certain sum is invested at 16% per annum compounded quarterly. It amounts to ₹11248.64 after 9 months. What was the principal?
questions.explanation: Let P be the principal, quarterly rate = 16/4 = 4% = 0.04 Number of quarters in 9 months = 3 Amount = P(1 + 0.04)³ = P X 1.124864 = 11248.64 P = 11248.64 / 1.124864 = 10000

questions.question: Three pipes - D, E and F fill a tank at 5 litres/min, 7 litres/min and 8 litres/min respectively. When all three pipes are opened together, they fill 45% of the tank in 9 minutes. What is the capacity of the tank in litres?
questions.explanation: Total filling rate when all three are opened together = 5 + 7 + 8 = 20 litres/min In 9 minutes, quantity of water filled = 20 9 = 180 litres Let, total tank capacity = x litres 45% of x = 180 => (45/100) * x = 180 => x = 400 litres

questions.question: A sector of a circular sheet of radius 35 cm and an arc length of 132 cm is cut and a cone is formed by joining the edges. What is the volume of the cone formed?
questions.explanation: ![](https://u1.oliveboard.in/exams/solution/oliveimg/p240/image008.jpg) Let h and r be the height and the base radius of the cone formed. 2πr = length of arc = 132 cm r = (132* 7)/44 = 21 cm As the slant height of the cone is the radius of the sheet, l = 35 cm Height of the cone (h) = √((l)²- (r)²) = √((35)² - (21)²) = √(1225-441) = √784 = 28 cm Therefore volume of the cone formed = ⅓ * π * (21)²* 28 = 12936 cu cm

questions.question: Ashish purchases 12 kg of type A rice at the price of Rs.45 per kg and 32 kg of type B rice at the price of Rs.60 per kg. He mixes both type of rice along with 6 kg of impurity and sold the complete mixture at Rs.59.04 per kg, then what is the profit percent earned by Ashish if the cost of impurity is zero (0)?
questions.explanation: Cost of 12 kg of type A rice = 12 * 45 = Rs.540 Cost of 32 kg of type B rice = 32 * 60 = Rs.1920 Cost of mixture = 540 + 1920 + 0 = Rs.2460 Selling price of mixture = (12 + 32 + 6) * 59.04 = Rs.2952 Profit amount = 2952 - 2460 = Rs.492 Per cent profit = (492/2460) * 100 = 20%

questions.question: O is the circumcenter of triangle ABC. If ∠OAC=25° then ∠ABC is equal to:
questions.explanation: O is the circumcenter of triangle ABC. So, AB will be the circle's diameter and C should be on the circle. And therefore, ∠ ACB = 90° Therefore, ∠ ABC = 180 - (90 + ∠OAC) = 180 - (90 + 25) = 65°

questions.question: The ratio of the 1ˢᵗ number to the 2ⁿᵈ number is 12: 13 and the square of the sum of the 2ⁿᵈ number and the 3ʳᵈ number is 2116. If the 3ʳᵈ number is 4 less than the 1ˢᵗ number, then the value of the 2ⁿᵈ number is:
questions.explanation: Let, 1ˢᵗ number and 2ⁿᵈ number be 12a and 13a respectively. 3ʳᵈ number = 12a - 4 According to question, [13a + (12a - 4)]² = 2116 => 25a - 4 = 46 => a = 2 Hence, 2ⁿᵈ number = 2 * 13 = 26

questions.question: Akhil decided to fill the floor of his square bed room using titles where each tile is a square of dimension 1 X 1 meters and the dimension of the bed room is 4 X 4 meters. He purchased white and orange colour tiles costing Rs.70 and Rs.60 respectively. All the white tiles are fitted along the edges of the bedroom and orange ones at the centre. What is the cost incurred while flooring the bed room?
questions.explanation: No of tiles required = 4x4 = 16. The arrangement of the tiles is shown as WWWW WOOW WOOW WWWW So there are 12 white and 4 orange tiles. Total cost incurred = 12 x 70 + 4 x 60 = Rs. 1080

questions.question: Simplify: tan²θ - sin²θ_________sec²θ - 1
questions.explanation: tan²θ - sin²θ = (sin²θ/cos²θ) - sin²θ = sin²θ (1/cos²θ - 1) = sin²θ (tan²θ) sec²θ - 1 = tan²θ ∴ (tan²θ - sin²θ)/(sec²θ - 1) = [sin²θ · tan²θ]/tan²θ = sin²θ

questions.question: Select the correctly spelt word to fill in the blank. She took his _________ as a sign that he felt better.
questions.explanation: The correct spelling is 'sarcasm'. 'Sarcasm' means the use of words or expressions to mean the opposite of what they actually say. People use sarcasm in order to criticize other people or to make them look silly. Hence, option (b) is correct.

questions.question: Select the most appropriate meaning of the underlined idiom in the given sentence. Henry finds it hard to wrap his mind around such complex issues.
options: To succeed in understanding something difficult
questions.explanation: The idiom 'wrap one's mind around' means 'to find a way to understand or accept (something)'. Hence, the answer is option c).

questions.question: The following sentence has been divided into four segments. Identify the segment that contains an error. The quality of the raw materials / and finished goods / are regularly tested / by the team.
options: The quality of the raw materials
questions.explanation: The subject is "The quality," which is singular. The phrase "of the raw materials and finished goods" does not affect the number of the subject. So, the verb should be "is" (singular) instead of "are" (plural). Correct sentence: The quality of the raw materials and finished goods is regularly tested by the team. So, there is an error in the part mentioned in option (c). Hence, option (c) is the answer.

questions.question: Choose the sentence that uses a homonym of the underlined word: The die was cast, and the game began.
options: The little boy insisted that only he throws the die.
He rolled the die across the board repeatedly.
A die has six faces with numbers on each face.
The flowers began to die after the first frost.
questions.explanation: In the original sentence, "die" is a noun meaning a game cube. Options a), b) and c) use "die" in the same sense. Option d) uses "die" as a verb meaning to perish, which is unrelated to object. Hence, option (d) is the correct answer.

questions.question: Which of the following sentences is grammatically correct?
options: I wish I can fly like a bird.
I wish I fly like a bird.
I wish I will fly like a bird.
I wish I could fly like a bird.
questions.explanation: The correct sentence is (d). "Wish" for unreal present uses past conditional "could" in the that-clause. Option (a) is incorrect because "can" is present; unreal wishes backshift to "could." Option (b) is incorrect because simple present "fly" doesn't backshift tense for wishes. Option (c) is incorrect as "will" is future, not fitting unreal present. Hence, the correct answer is option (d).

questions.question: The following sentence has been split into four segments. Identify the segment that contains a grammatical error. The success of the project will be guaranteed if everyone followed the schedule diligently.
questions.explanation: The segment that contains a grammatical error is 'If everyone followed'. The verb "followed" is in the past tense, but the sentence discusses a future condition. In the sentence, "will be guaranteed" indicates a future consequence ("will be") based on a condition ("if everyone followed"). Therefore, the correct verb form to use in this future condition is "follows" (present tense), not "followed" (past tense). Hence, (d) is the correct answer. The correct sentence is: The success of the project will be guaranteed if everyone follows the schedule diligently.

questions.question: Select the option that will improve the underlined part of the given sentence. In case no improvement is needed, select 'No improvement required'. She can able to speak several languages.
questions.explanation: Both 'able to' and 'could/can' show capacity. So, they are never used together in a sentence as together they make the sentence superfluous. So, the use of 'can speak' is appropriate here. Hence, option (a) is correct. The correct sentence is: She can speak several languages.

questions.question: Fill in the blank using the verb (in bracket) in future perfect continuous tense. By next year, she __________ in this company for five years. (work)
questions.explanation: The future perfect continuous tense is formed using: "will have been" + present participle (verb + -ing). The phrase "By next year" indicates a duration of time continuing up to a point in the future, which fits the future perfect continuous tense. Thus, the correct sentence is: "By next year, she will have been working in this company for five years." Option a): "will work" is simple future, incorrect. Option b): "will be working" is future continuous, incorrect. Option d): "has been working" is present perfect continuous, incorrect. Hence, option (c) is the answer.

questions.question: Select the most appropriate option to fill in the blank. Working for the United Nations, she was an invaluable asset as she could fluently speak and understand over seven different languages. Such a person is known as a ________.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is d) Polyglot. Context: The sentence describes a person who has the ability to speak many languages. · Polyglot: This term specifically refers to a person who knows and is able to use several languages. This is the perfect word for the person described. · Linguist: This is a person who scientifically studies language and its structure. While a linguist might be a polyglot (and vice versa), their primary definition relates to the study of language, not necessarily the ability to speak many. · Orator: This is a skilled public speaker. This is incorrect. · Lexicographer: This is a person who compiles dictionaries. This is incorrect.

questions.question: Find the synonym of "Recalcitrant".
questions.explanation: The correct answer is B) Obstinate. The word recalcitrant refers to a person who is stubbornly resistant to authority, rules, or instructions. Similarly, obstinate means unwilling to change one's opinion or behavior, especially despite advice or warnings. The other options suggest willingness or flexibility, which is the opposite of the given word.

questions.question: Select the option that expresses the given sentence in active voice. Can the home be renovated by the engineer?
options: Can the engineer be renovate the home?
Can the engineer renovated the home?
Could the engineer renovate the home?
Can the engineer renovate the home?
questions.explanation: The changes that happen while converting Passive voice sentence to Active voice sentence are: the Subject in the Passive voice becomes the Object in the Active voice and the Object in the Passive voice will become the Subject in the Active voice. The helping verb [be] and the preposition 'by' should be removed while changing from Passive voice to Active voice. The given passive voice sentence is an Interrogative sentence. The answer to this question will be either "yes" or "no". These types of sentences usually begin with a Modal Auxiliary Verb (Can). The Passive voice format is Modal + Object of the Active voice (Subjective case) + be + V3 + by + Subject of the Active voice (Objective case). The Active voice format is Modal + Subject + V1 + Object. For example: Can the destination be reached on time by him? (Passive voice) Can he reach the destination on time? (Active voice) From the given options, the one that follows the above-mentioned changes is option (d), "Can the engineer renovate the home?" Hence, the correct answer is option (d).

questions.question: Pick the spelling that is correct for the ability to judge well or make sound decisions.
questions.explanation: Solution: Among the given options, "discernment" is the correct spelling. The other options involve incorrect vowel or consonant placement. Hence, (b) is the correct answer.

questions.question: Choose the correct synonym of the word underlined in the sentence from the alternatives given below it. They decided to negotiate terms to reach a compromise.
questions.explanation: 'Negotiate' is a verb that means to engage in discussions or formal talks in order to reach an agreement, especially in a situation where there are conflicting interests or differences. (a) Search: "Search" means to look for or explore something. (b) Discuss: This is the correct synonym. "Discuss" means to talk about a topic, exchange ideas, or consider different aspects of a matter. It aligns with the concept of negotiating terms and reaching a compromise. (c) Cause: "Cause" refers to something that produces an effect or result. (d) Highlight: "Highlight" means to emphasize or draw attention to something. Hence, (b) is the correct answer.

questions.question: Select the option that can be used as a one-word substitute for the given group of words. An intense dislike or fear of people from other countries or cultures
questions.explanation: Xenophobia: An irrational dislike, fear, or hatred of people from other countries or cultures. Philanthropy: The desire to promote the welfare of others, usually through generous donations of money, time, or resources. Nationalism: Strong identification with and pride in one's own nation, sometimes to the exclusion or detriment of other nations. Cosmopolitanism: The idea that all human beings belong to a single community, regardless of nationality or culture. So, "an intense dislike or fear of people from other countries or cultures" is best described as "xenophobia". Hence, option (c) is the answer.

questions.question: Select the most appropriate meaning of the given idiom. Cut someone some slack.
options: To treat a person with respect.
questions.explanation: The meaning of the idiom 'cut someone some slack' is 'treat (someone) in a less harsh or critical way'. Accordingly, the correct answer is given in option (a), 'To treat someone less harshly'. Hence, the correct answer is option (a).

questions.question: Sentences of a paragraph are given below in jumbled order. Arrange the sentences in the correct order to form a meaningful and coherent paragraph. P. His uncle was large and neck-less, with an enormous black moustache; and his aunt was horse-faced and bony. Q. Tom looked nothing like the rest of the family. R. He wore round glasses, and on his forehead was a thin scar. S. Tom, on the other hand, was small and skinny, with brilliant blue eyes and jet-black hair that was always untidy.
questions.explanation: The first statement cannot be S because the use of the phrase "on the other hand" requires a prior statement to indicate a different idea. So, we can eliminate option d). Based on a similar reason, S cannot immediately follow Q because S and Q offer no opposing opinions or ideas. So, we can eliminate options a) and b) as well. If we look at the arrangement QPSR, the flow of the sentences is contextually correct. Q starts with the fact that Tom looks different from his family members. This is followed by P which describes the physical appearances of his uncle and aunt. Then, S shows the opposing features of Tom. R concludes the paragraph. (Here, R does not precede S because of the use of the phrase 'on the other hand' in S, connecting it to P). Therefore, the correct answer is QPSR i.e. option c).

questions.question: Choose the word that best fits blank (1):
questions.explanation: The sentence introduces both positive and negative aspects of digital transformation. "Heralds" means announces or signals the arrival of - it fits the tone of something significant and dual-natured arriving now. "Conceals" means hides. "Reverses" means turns back. "Postpones" means delays. The transformation is actively happening, not hidden or delayed. Hence, the correct answer is option (a).

questions.question: The word "automate" most nearly means:
options: Converting a manual process to a completely self-reliant process
Moving from a tensed to a relaxed situation
questions.explanation: The word "automate" means convert (a process or facility) to be operated by largely automatic equipment. Accordingly, the option that is closest in meaning is " Converting a manual process to a completely self-reliant process." Hence, the correct answer is option (a).

questions.question: The word "displace" most nearly means:
options: Taking over the function of an existing process
questions.explanation: The word "displace" means take over the place, position, or role of. Accordingly, the option that is closest in meaning is "taking over the function of an existing process." Hence, the correct answer is option (c).

questions.question: Choose the word that best fits blank (4):
questions.explanation: The gig economy and remote work have changed old ideas of security and loyalty negatively. "Eroded" means gradually destroyed or weakened - fits perfectly. "Reinforced" and "preserved" mean strengthened. "Established" means created firmly. Hence, the correct answer is option (b).

questions.question: Choose the word that best fits blank (5):
questions.explanation: The passage warns that unemployment could harm "social cohesion and widen economic divides." "Undermine" means weaken or damage - matches the negative consequence. The other options suggest positive or neutral effects. Hence, the correct answer is option (d).

questions.question: Read the following conversation and choose the correct indirect form of the sentence given below. The teacher said to the students,"You have not completed your assignment on time. If you do not submit it by tomorrow, I will take strict action against you."
options: The teacher told the students that they had not completed their assignment on time and if they did not submit it by the next day, he would take strict action against them.
The teacher said to the students that you had not completed your assignment on time and if you do not submit it by tomorrow, I will take strict action against you.
The teacher told the students that they have not completed their assignment on time and if they do not submit it by the next day, he will take strict action against them.
The teacher said that the students had not completed their assignment on time and if they would not submit it by tomorrow, he will take strict action against them.
questions.explanation: In indirect speech, said to changes into told, and the reporting verb being in the past tense requires a backshift of tenses. The present perfect tense "have not completed" changes into "had not completed". The future tense "will take" changes into "would take". Pronouns and time expressions are also changed: you → they and tomorrow → the next day. Only option A correctly applies all these rules.

questions.question: Select the most appropriate ANTONYM of the given word. Exuberant
questions.explanation: Exuberant - full of energy, excitement, and cheerfulness. Gloomy - causing or feeling depression or despondency. So, both words are opposite in meaning. Hence, option (c) is the answer. Peppy - lively and high-spirited. Sunny - bright with sunlight. Cheerful - noticeably happy and optimistic.

questions.question: Select the most appropriate word to fill in the blank We're all familiar with the mind-bending ______ of psychedelic drugs.
questions.explanation: A noun is needed in the blank to be modified by the adjective phrase 'mind-bending'. 'Ordinary' is an adjective hence eliminated. Blemish- a small mark or flaw which spoils the appearance of something; this does not provide a logical meaning to the sentence. 'Properties' means 'an attribute, quality, or characteristic of something'; this is the correct answer. The sentence talks about mind-bending characteristics of psychedelic drugs. Hence, (c) is the right answer.

questions.question: Choose the antonym of "Permanent".
questions.explanation: The word "permanent" means lasting for a long time or forever. (a) "Temporary" means lasting for a short time and directly contrasts with permanence. (b) "Stable" means steady or unchanging and is similar in sense to "permanent". (c) "Distant" refers to space or relationship and is unrelated to duration. (d) "Polite" describes good manners and has no connection with time. Thus, "temporary" is the correct antonym of "permanent", making option (a) the correct choice.
